mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint increases in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals rapid supply-side recovery, which immediately improves market sentiment and triggers positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO spikes do not lead to positive 5-day returns in recovery regimes, with win rate below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 240, entrenched inflation forces aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 240 coincide with positive 20-day returns in >55% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate indicates robust job creation and tightening labor markets, which supports consumer spending and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings and generates positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth does not correlate with positive 60-day returns, with Rank IC remaining negative over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a deceleration in the pace of federal funds rate increases signals policy relief, which reduces discount rate pressure and triggers positive short-term equity rallies.
failure_condition: Deceleration in rate hikes leads to negative 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances.
